questions.text: Heat is a form of:
questions.explanation: Heat is the transfer of thermal energy between systems.

questions.text: The standard unit of temperature is:
questions.explanation: Kelvin is the SI unit for temperature.

questions.text: Which type of thermometer is used to measure body temperature?
questions.explanation: Clinical thermometers have a kink to prevent mercury from falling back, allowing accurate reading.

questions.text: The normal body temperature of a healthy human being is:
questions.explanation: In Fahrenheit, it is 98.6°F.

questions.text: The transfer of heat from a hotter part to a colder part by the actual movement of particles is called:
questions.explanation: Convection occurs in fluids (liquids and gases) due to density differences.

questions.text: The transfer of heat from a hotter part to a colder part without any actual movement of particles is called:
questions.explanation: Conduction is common in solids, where heat is transferred through vibrations of particles.

questions.text: The transfer of heat that does not require any medium is called:
questions.explanation: Radiation can travel through a vacuum, like heat from the sun reaching Earth.

questions.text: Materials that allow heat to pass through them easily are called:
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors of heat.

questions.text: Materials that do NOT allow heat to pass through them easily are called:
questions.explanation: Wood, plastic, and air are examples of insulators.

questions.text: Which of the following is the best conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Metals, especially copper, are excellent conductors of heat.

questions.text: Sea breeze blows during the:
questions.explanation: During the day, land heats up faster than the sea, causing air above land to rise and cooler air from the sea to move in.

questions.text: Land breeze blows during the:
questions.explanation: At night, land cools faster than the sea, causing air above the sea to rise and cooler air from land to move in.

questions.text: Dark coloured surfaces absorb ______ heat than light coloured surfaces.
questions.explanation: Dark colours are good absorbers of heat, which is why we prefer light clothes in summer.

questions.text: Light coloured surfaces reflect ______ heat than dark coloured surfaces.
questions.explanation: Light colours are good reflectors of heat, keeping us cooler.

questions.text: The range of a clinical thermometer is:
questions.explanation: This range is sufficient for measuring human body temperature.

questions.text: The range of a laboratory thermometer is typically:
questions.explanation: Laboratory thermometers are designed for a wider range of temperatures in experiments.

questions.text: Which of the following is an insulator?
questions.explanation: Wood does not conduct heat well.

questions.text: The process of heat transfer in liquids and gases is mainly by:
questions.explanation: Convection currents are formed due to the movement of fluid particles.

questions.text: The process of heat transfer in solids is mainly by:
questions.explanation: Particles vibrate and transfer energy to adjacent particles.

questions.text: Which of the following is a good absorber of heat?
questions.explanation: Black surfaces absorb most of the incident radiation.

questions.text: Woollen clothes keep us warm in winter because:
options: Wool is a good conductor of heat
Wool is an insulator and traps air
questions.explanation: Trapped air is a poor conductor of heat, preventing heat loss from our bodies.

questions.text: The kink in a clinical thermometer prevents:
options: Mercury level from falling on its own
questions.explanation: This allows the thermometer to be read accurately after removal from the patient.

questions.text: Which of these is a poor conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Water is a relatively poor conductor compared to metals.

questions.text: The transfer of heat from the sun to the Earth is primarily by:
questions.explanation: Space is a vacuum, so heat travels as electromagnetic waves (radiation).

questions.text: Temperature is a measure of the ______ of an object.
questions.explanation: Higher temperature means higher average kinetic energy of the particles.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a unit of heat energy?
questions.explanation: Celsius is a unit of temperature, not energy.

questions.text: A hot object loses heat to a cold object until:
options: Both objects have the same mass
Both objects have the same temperature
questions.explanation: Heat transfer continues until thermal equilibrium is reached.

questions.text: Why are cooking utensils made of metal and have plastic handles?
options: Metals are cheap, plastic is expensive
Metals are good conductors, plastic is an insulator
Metals are light, plastic is heavy
Metals are flexible, plastic is rigid
questions.explanation: Metal conducts heat to cook food, while plastic handles prevent burns.

questions.text: The process of heat transfer in boiling water is primarily by:
questions.explanation: Convection currents distribute heat throughout the water.

questions.text: Which type of clothes are preferred in summer?
questions.explanation: Light colours reflect heat, and cotton is breathable, keeping us cool.

questions.text: The lowest temperature on the Celsius scale is 0°C, which corresponds to the freezing point of water. What is the highest temperature on the Celsius scale?
questions.explanation: 100°C is the boiling point of water at standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.text: A bimetallic strip works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Different metals expand at different rates when heated, causing the strip to bend.

questions.text: Why are houses in hot climates often painted white?
questions.explanation: White surfaces reflect sunlight, keeping the interior cooler.

questions.text: The instrument used to measure temperature is called a:
questions.explanation: A thermometer measures temperature.

questions.text: Which of the following is a good conductor of electricity but a poor conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Graphite (a form of carbon) conducts electricity well but is a relatively poor thermal conductor.

questions.text: The process of heat transfer from a hot object to a cold object is always:
questions.explanation: Heat naturally flows from higher temperature to lower temperature.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the vacuum in a thermos flask?
questions.explanation: Vacuum is an excellent insulator as it prevents heat transfer by conduction and convection.

questions.text: The shiny inner surface of a thermos flask helps to reduce heat transfer by:
questions.explanation: Shiny surfaces are poor emitters and good reflectors of thermal radiation.

questions.text: Which of these is an example of heat transfer by convection?
questions.explanation: Boiling water involves the movement of heated water particles.

questions.text: The SI unit of heat energy is:
questions.explanation: Joule is the standard international unit for all forms of energy, including heat.
